decision_gate.gate_summary: Passes on liquidity and solvency, but significant caution on structural SBC (40%+ of revenue). High quality cash generation protects the downside.
decision_gate.variant_perception_summary: Market views SNOW as a commoditizing data warehouse facing competition from Databricks and hyperscalers. We view it as a 'Data Gravity' platform with an emerging AI ecosystem moat (Cortex/OpenAI collaboration).
decision_gate.why_continue_or_stop: Continue. 40%+ revenue growth and highly negative accruals indicate institutional-grade fundamental quality despite GAAP losses.

recommendation.thesis_expiration: 2029-03-11 or if FCF growth drops below 20% YoY.

three_line_thesis: Data gravity ensures customers remain locked into the Snowflake ecosystem as their data volumes scale.
Transition from a query engine to an AI-native data platform provides a new growth S-curve.
Institutional-grade cash flow (OCF positive) allows for massive reinvestment or buybacks to offset dilution.
thesis.market_mispricing: Market is over-penalizing near-term growth deceleration and ignoring the stickiness of the underlying data platform.
must_be_true: Customers continue to prefer Snowflake's abstraction over managing their own cloud infrastructure.
SBC as % of revenue trends down to <20% over 5 years.
AI Cortex adoption drives incremental compute spend.
thesis.bear_case_steelman: Hyperscalers (AWS/Azure) integrate their native data tools so tightly that third-party platforms like Snowflake become unnecessary overhead. Open-source formats (Iceberg) break the lock-in.
thesis.why_market_may_be_right: Valuation (72x forward PE) leaves zero room for error in execution or macro headwinds.
three_ways_i_am_wrong: Iceberg tables successfully commoditize the storage layer.
Databricks wins the unified AI/Data workload battle.
Compute pricing pressures compress margins permanently.

business_quality.competitive_position_notes: Leading independent cloud data platform; faces pressure from 'good enough' native cloud tools.

valuation.peer_multiples_summary: Trading at a significant premium to general SaaS (8x sales) but in-line with hyper-growth infrastructure peers.

key_assumptions: Market implies 22% FCF growth for 10 years

risk.what_breaks_first_under_stress: Enterprise cloud optimization projects during a recession leading to seat/compute reduction.

missing_items: Exact NRR for mid-market vs enterprise
